initial_prompt:
Super-Eywa v1 runtime prompt snapshot.
Turn index: 1
Node depth: 0
Allowed decisions: ['delegate']
Prompt family: delegate
Base header prompt: ''
Selected prompt text:
Decompose the problem into exactly one or two highly impactful subproblems for other agents. Each helper must be assigned a distinct, non-overlapping responsibility (such as a specific algorithmic component or a geometric optimization routine) that is essential for the final solution. Avoid high-level strategy summaries; define specific logic or mathematical tasks for each helper to ensure their outputs are actionable. 

Strictly limit the number of helpers to a maximum of 2.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "delegate",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "helpers": [
    {
      "label": "helper_1",
      "instructions": "plain-text subproblem for the first helper"
    }
  ],
  "synthesis_brief": "how the parent should combine the child results"
}
Additional instruction prompt profiles: []
System prompt:


User prompt:
Decompose the problem into exactly one or two highly impactful subproblems for other agents. Each helper must be assigned a distinct, non-overlapping responsibility (such as a specific algorithmic component or a geometric optimization routine) that is essential for the final solution. Avoid high-level strategy summaries; define specific logic or mathematical tasks for each helper to ensure their outputs are actionable. 

Strictly limit the number of helpers to a maximum of 2.

Question:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-S1-ahc001-atcoder-ad
Title: S1. AHC001 / AtCoder Ad

Problem:
Place many non-overlapping axis-aligned ad rectangles in a 10000 x 10000 square so each advertiser gets a rectangle containing its requested point and with area close to its requested target area.

Grading target:
Continuous score from advertiser satisfaction.
Higher is better.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
final_prompt:
Super-Eywa v1 runtime prompt snapshot.
Turn index: 2
Node depth: 0
Allowed decisions: ['execute_locally']
Prompt family: delegate
Base header prompt: ''
Selected prompt text:
Child results have already come back to you. Use them to produce the final response yourself. Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "execute_locally",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "response": "string",
  "result_type": "optional string"
}
Additional instruction prompt profiles: []
System prompt:


User prompt:
Child results have already come back to you. Use them to produce the final response yourself. Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "execute_locally",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "response": "string",
  "result_type": "optional string"
}

Synthesis brief from the earlier child-creation step:
The parent agent will take the initial spatial partitions from the spatial_partitioner and pass the resulting bounding boxes and advertiser constraints to the area_optimizer. The final output will be the set of optimized rectangle coordinates (x1, y1, x2, y2) for each advertiser.

Child results already returned to you:
[
  {
    "node_id": "node_root_helper_01",
    "summary": "FINAL_ANSWER: A k-d tree or quadtree algorithm\nJUSTIFICATION: These algorithms recursively partition space based on point density."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "node_root_helper_02",
    "summary": "FINAL_ANSWER: Local optimization of rectangle dimensions\nJUSTIFICATION: Adjust dimensions to match target area within cell constraints."
  }
]
